sections.paragraph:
νήθω formed fr. νέω (q.v.) as πλήθω fr. πλη-, πίμπλημι (s. L-S-J-M νήθω); aor. 3 pl. ἔνησαν Ex 35:26; pf. pass. ptc. νενησμένος LXX (Cratinus Com. 96; Pla., Pol 289c; Anth. Pal. 11, 110, 6; 14, 134, 3; Ex 26:31 al. in Ex; TestSol 4:12) spin w. κοπιάω Mt 6:28; Lk 12:27. W. ὑφαίνω 12:27 D. For GJs 10:2 s. νέω.—B. 408.—Dalman, Arbeit V ’37.—DELG s.v. 2 νέω. M-M.

  1. fr. = from

q.v. q.v. = quod vide (which see, see that which has just been mentioned)

L-S-J-M L-S-J-M = (L-S-J) HLiddell/RScott, A Greek-English Lexicon; New Edition by HSJones/RMcKenzie—List 6

aor. aor. = aorist

  1. pl. = plural
  2. pf. = perfect

pass. pass. = passive (either of grammatical form or of passive experience); also used in reference to literary portion=passage

ptc. ptc. = participle

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

Cratinus Cratinus Com , V b.c.—List 5

Pla Pla , V–IV b.c.; s. also Ps.-Plato—List 5

Pol Pol = Polycarp to the Philippians—List 1

  1. al. =alibi (elsewhere), aliter (otherwise), alii (others)

TestSol TestSol = Testament of Solomon, I–III a.d.—List 2

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

GJs GJs = Gospel of James (Protevangelium Jacobi), II a.d.—List 1

  1. B. = CDBuck, A Dictionary of Selected Synonyms—List 6

Dalman Dalman = GD., various works—List 6

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 671
